Confidential — Indicative Term Sheet: Proposed Acquisition of HelixGrid Oy by Stonebridge Renewables N.V.
Issuer: Stonebridge Renewables N.V., admitted to trading on the Aurelia Stock Exchange (ASE) Regulated Market; Target: HelixGrid Oy (private).
Structure: Acquisition of 70% of issued shares at signing with a call option for the remaining 30% exercisable 24–36 months post-closing.
Consideration: €148m cash at closing plus €32m earn-out; c.22% of Issuer’s last reported market capitalisation, assessed for issuer‑relative materiality and likely to be price-sensitive.
Timetable: Expected signing 28 June 2026; closing in Q4 2026, subject to Aurelia Financial Markets Authority (AFMA) merger clearance and customary approvals; this document constitutes inside information under EU MAR Art 7.
Disclosure: Disclosure delayed under MAR Art 17(4) until signing; regulated information to be disseminated via ASE RIS by 07:30 CET on the next trading day or earlier if confidentiality cannot be ensured.
Insider controls: PDMR dealing prohibited during MAR closed periods and throughout a transaction-specific blackout window; Issuer to maintain an insider list per MAR Art 18 and leak‑response protocols.
Public reference (press): The Issuer’s 5 April 2026 press release stating it was “considering smart‑grid opportunities” is public via the Aurelia OAM and does not disclose the Target, price, or terms.
Public reference (filing): The Issuer’s 2025 Annual Report filed with the Aurelia OAM on 20 February 2026 is public and contains no reference to this proposed transaction.
Selective disclosure/market soundings: Limited to lenders and advisers under NDA; any market soundings to comply with MAR Art 11 with contemporaneous records and wall‑crossing consents.
Sustainability undertakings: Post‑closing, Target to align with Issuer’s CSRD reporting and SFDR PAI indicators; no processing of special‑category personal data (GDPR Art 9) other than aggregated, anonymised workforce statistics for reporting.
Data transfers: Cross‑border transfers of personal data outside the EEA (to processors in Norlandia) to rely on SCCs with supplementary measures; clean‑team protocols apply to employee‑level datasets in the data room.
Conditions precedent: AFMA merger clearance, employee information/consultation where required, no MAC, customary warranties, completion accounts in €, and notifications required by the ASE Rulebook.
Financing: Funded from existing cash and a committed €120m revolving facility from Venturo Bank S.A. (fictional), which has received only high‑level, non‑inside information to date.
Benign operational note: The Issuer’s canteen refurbishment next week is unrelated to this transaction and is not price‑sensitive.
Contacts: Ms. Dr. Livia Brandt, General Counsel, l.brandt@stonebridgeren.example; Mr. Paolo Ricci, Head of Investor Relations, ir@stonebridgeren.example.
